issue_summary: Allow stopping condition based on the number of iterations (for "univariate" optimizers).
javadoc_before:
/**
 * Simple implementation of the
 * {@link org.apache.commons.math3.optimization.ConvergenceChecker} interface
 * that uses only objective function values.
 *
 * Convergence is considered to have been reached if either the relative
 * difference between the objective function values is smaller than a
 * threshold or if either the absolute difference between the objective
 * function values is smaller than another threshold.
 *
 * @version $Id$
 * @since 3.1
 */
javadoc_after:
/** * Simple implementation of the * {@link org.apache.commons.math3.optimization.ConvergenceChecker} interface * that uses only objective function values. * * Convergence is considered to have been reached if either the relative * difference between the objective function values is smaller than a * threshold or if either the absolute difference between the objective * function values is smaller than another threshold. *
* The {@link #converged(int,UnivariatePointValuePair,UnivariatePointValuePair) * converged} method will also return {@code true} if the number of iterations * has been set (see {@link #SimpleUnivariateValueChecker(double,double,int) * this constructor}). * * @version $Id$ * @since 3.1 */
